---
title: "Welcome"
---

## What this is

This is an interactive course on generative modeling built around three families of models:

- **VAEs** — variational inference, the ELBO, and why Gaussian decoders blur
- **GANs** — the minimax game, optimal discriminator, and measurable mode collapse
- **Diffusion** — score matching, DDPM forward/reverse derivations, and sampling

Every figure is tied to a **measurable claim**: we derive the math, implement it, and verify it numerically. No cherry-picked sample strips.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+ and basic NumPy / PyTorch familiarity
- Comfort with probability (expectations, KL divergence) at an introductory ML level
- A laptop CPU is enough — all models are small MLPs on 8×8 digits and finish in a few minutes

## Setup

Create a virtual environment and install dependencies:

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Install [Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/download/) (one-time):

```bash
# macOS
brew install --cask quarto

# or download the installer from https://quarto.org/docs/download/
```

## How to use these materials

**Read the rendered book** (uses cached notebook outputs — fast):

```bash
quarto render
open _book/index.html
```

**Live preview** while editing:

```bash
quarto preview
```

**Run the notebook interactively** (re-executes all cells):

```bash
jupyter lab generative_first_principles.ipynb
```

Or re-render with fresh execution:

```bash
quarto render --execute
```

## Tips for students

| Variable | Default | Effect |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `QUICK` | `True` in source build | Faster training; set `False` for nicer figures |
| `SEED` | `0` | Reproducible runs |
| `IMG` | `8` | Image side length (8×8 digits) |

The notebook source is generated by `build_notebook.py`. After editing that file, run:

```bash
python build_notebook.py
quarto render
```

## Structure

| Part | Topic |
|------|-------|
| 0 | One unifying framework — what object are we learning? |
| 1 | VAE — ELBO derivation, latent diagnostics, L2 blur, VQ-VAE |
| 2 | GAN — minimax, JS divergence, measured collapse |
| 3 | Denoising score matching |
| 4 | DDPM — forward/reverse derived, ancestral + DDIM sampling |
| 5 | Quantitative comparison + modern diffusion family |
| Appendix | Common misconceptions, each linked to a cell |

Continue to the [full notebook](generative_first_principles.html) →
